preamble: The voiced alveolar fricative [z] surfaces in French liaison before vowel-initial words. Strictly speaking, this is not true epenthesis (∅→z) but the resurfacing of a historically present but normally silent final /z/ (from historical -s/-x spellings). Functionally it resolves vowel hiatus.

examples.note: Liaison: latent /z/ from the -s of 'les' surfaces before vowel-initial words. Historical final [z] preserved as liaison consonant.
Liaison: final -x of 'deux' (historically /z/) surfaces before vowel. Not true ∅→z insertion.
